history_of_present_illness.definition: A tummy tuck - surgery that removes excess lower abdominal skin and tightens the abdominal wall.
Separation of the two main tummy muscles, often after pregnancy. It is repaired during a tummy tuck.
Soft tubes that let extra fluid drain from the wound while it heals. The nurse removes them once output is low.

physical_exam.definition: The belly button, which is reshaped and repositioned during a tummy tuck.
A collection of blood under the skin. Sudden severe swelling or pressure at the wound can signal this and needs urgent review.
Soft tubes draining fluid from the wound. Recording the output helps decide when they can come out.

assessment.definition: When a wound edge starts to open or separate. This is one of the signs to report to the practice.
A pocket of clear fluid that can gather under a healing wound. Compression garments help reduce it.

plan.definition: An antibiotic given for a short course after surgery to help prevent wound infection.
A blood-thinning injection that lowers the risk of clots (DVT) while you are less mobile.
A firm supportive garment worn day and night. It reduces swelling and supports the healing tissues.
